probe_method: Send requests with model-family-specific stop tokens. Verify output terminates at stop token (not visible in output, done_reason=stop). Test custom stop token passthrough.

probes.note: With num_predict=500, response was empty — Qwen thinking-mode consumed token budget. With num_predict=2000, clean 487-char response. ~150 thinking tokens consumed internally.
Custom stop token 'dogs' triggered within Qwen's thinking content (invisible to user), producing empty visible output. Stop mechanism works but matches against thinking tokens.

probes.response_preview: Cats are independent and curious creatures...

probes.note: GLM failed to load (HTTP 500). Cannot test stop token handling.

evidence_summary: Stop tokens work correctly for all 3 loadable models. Native stop tokens are not visible in output. Custom stop tokens are passed through and respected. Qwen caveat: custom stop tokens match against thinking-mode content, which can cause premature termination of visible output. This is a Qwen-specific behavior, not an Ollama bug.
w2_recommendation: Dispatcher should use model-family-specific stop tokens (not Qwen tokens for all models). Qwen num_predict should account for thinking-mode overhead (~150-200 extra tokens).
